obligation_text: Deployers who are public authorities or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall register and select the system and register its use in the EU database. Other deployers shall be authorised to register themselves and select the system and register its use.
scoring_text: Knowledge of deployer registration obligations in the EU AI database, including system selection documentation, use case registration, and differentiated requirements for public authorities versus other deployers.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of deployer registration obligations in the EU AI database, including system selection documentation, use case registration, and differentiated requirements for public authorities versus other deployers.
reference_document_element_description: Prepare and submit regulatory filings, registrations, and notifications required by applicable data protection and AI regulations, including processing registrations, DPO appointments, and AI system registrations.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 49(4) (competency) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-010 is the strongest match (STS: 0.540, cosine: 0.639). Secondary: AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.624), AG-K-005 (AG, cosine: 0.623).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.539). Shared vocabulary: [ai, system]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [authorities, case, database, deployer, deployers, differentiated] vs WIDAI terms [applicable, appointments, data, dpo, filings, notifications] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.876, R=0.886). Balanced P/R — the 26-word EU AI Act competency and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.639 > STS 0.539 (gap +0.100). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, system]. EU AI Act-only: [authorities, case, database, deployer, deployers, differentiated]. WIDAI-only: [applicable, appointments, data, dpo, filings, notifications].
